title: cytisus supinus subsp. pallidus
intro: Cytisus supinus subsp. pallidus, also known as the pale kidney vetch, is a sub-species of the cytisus supinus plant, belonging to the legume family. It is a perennial herb native to the Iberian Peninsula, and traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The pale kidney vetch has been found to have anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing blood pressure and alleviating urinary tract issues. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and dried extracts, making it a popular choice for traditional herbal remedies.
